brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is a forward-process reinforcement-learning/policy-improvement objective (DiffusionNFT) applied to a learned velocity-field generator, using the MeanFlow identity to turn average velocities into an instantaneous-velocity predictor for reward optimization; Paper B's machinery is sequential Bayesian estimation of basin-of-attraction probabilities via a Dirichlet-multinomial prior update under parameter continuation, with a log Bayes factor sensing bifurcations to trigger adaptive resampling. These do not correspond role-for-role: RL reward/policy-gradient optimization of a generative velocity field is a different process from Bayesian posterior updating of attractor-convergence probabilities, and the only shared terms ('velocity', 'few steps'/'efficiency') are lexical rather than the same governing mechanism.
